label: Network-SINDy
description: Sparse network dynamics identification with explicit self term f(x_i) and coupling kernel g(x_i,x_j), evaluated with chronological 70/30 train-validation split. STLSQ is the sparse thresholded least-squares solver used internally.

interpretation: Among the 200-node results, this model reaches the lowest validation error but uses a more involved coupling kernel than the simplest alternatives.
The coupling term combines linear activity-difference drive with nonlinear correction, suggesting that upstream influence is not purely diffusive.
Its meaning is that local circuit propagation is still dominated by inter-neuron activity differences, but the effect bends nonlinearly when source and target activity diverge.

variables.name: x_i
variables.description: Activity of neuron i at the current time.

variables.name: W_{ij}
variables.description: Normalized incoming weight written with indices ij, where W_{ij} denotes a directed influence from neuron j to neuron i.

variables.name: f(x_i)
variables.description: Self-dynamics term capturing intrinsic recovery and nonlinear response of neuron i.

variables.name: g(x_i,x_j)
variables.description: Pairwise coupling kernel describing how activity difference between neurons j and i drives propagation.

interpretation: This is the most concise 200-node Network-SINDy result and nearly matches the best validation error.
The interaction kernel contains both a simple activity-difference term and a state-dependent modulation x_i(x_j-x_i), meaning neighbor influence depends on the target neuron's current state.
Dynamically, this implies signal propagation is not uniform: the same upstream input can have different impact depending on how active the receiving neuron already is.

variables.name: g(x_i,x_j)
variables.description: Pairwise coupling kernel describing how activity difference between neurons j and i drives propagation.

interpretation: This 200-node joint-Granger result is usable, but both error and kernel complexity are slightly less favorable than the Gaussian TE and DBN alternatives.
Its coupling kernel mixes linear, trigonometric, and state-dependent terms, pointing to a more heterogeneous interaction law.
The biological reading is that activity transfer is still difference-driven, but the inferred Granger network requires more nonlinear compensation to explain temporal evolution.

variables.name: x_i
variables.description: Activity of neuron i at the current time.

variables.name: W_{ij}
variables.description: Normalized incoming weight written with indices ij, where W_{ij} denotes a directed influence from neuron j to neuron i.

variables.name: f(x_i)
variables.description: Self-dynamics term capturing intrinsic recovery and nonlinear response of neuron i.

variables.name: g(x_i,x_j)
variables.description: Pairwise coupling kernel describing how activity difference between neurons j and i drives propagation.

interpretation: This is the strongest Network-SINDy candidate in the document: lowest validation error among the 500-node models and a very compact coupling kernel.
Its meaning is clear: propagation behaves like diffusive coupling driven by activity differences, plus a sinusoidal nonlinear correction that modulates transfer when phase or activity offset grows.

Compared with the earlier symbolic-regression results, this formulation more explicitly separates intrinsic neuron dynamics f(x_i) from network-mediated transmission g(x_i,x_j), which makes the circuit-level interpretation cleaner.

variables.name: g(x_i,x_j)
variables.description: Pairwise coupling kernel describing how activity difference between neurons j and i drives propagation.

interpretation: This model remains interpretable but is less compact than the DBN 500-node result.
The extra term x_i(x_j-x_i) means coupling is state-dependent: the same incoming difference can amplify or dampen differently depending on the target neuron's current level.
Its dynamical meaning is that the network may operate in a gated regime where propagation depends on both relative activity and the receiver's internal state.

variables.name: g(x_i,x_j)
variables.description: Pairwise coupling kernel describing how activity difference between neurons j and i drives propagation.

interpretation: Validation error is competitive with the best 500-node model, but the coupling kernel is much more complex.
This indicates the joint-Granger structure can also support a low-error network dynamics model, but only by invoking stronger nonlinear corrections and higher-order difference terms.
The safe interpretation is that activity-difference-driven coupling is still supported, but the exact coefficient-level mechanism is less robust and less suitable as the main reporting formula.
